reasoning: Both LLM fidelity and programmatic signals identified major issues with this document's conversion. The extraction dropped double asterisks used to denote 'Probable Action Items,' nested lists were flattened, and footnotes were incorrectly converted into bulleted lists. These structural and content alterations require human review.

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: The source document uses a double asterisk (`**`) immediately following specific agenda items to designate them as 'Probable Action Items' (as defined by the legend at the bottom of the pages). The extraction silently dropped these asterisks from multiple items, notably in the agenda schedules on both page 1 and page 2, altering the informational meaning of those items.
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
Approval of Agenda **
Approval of Meeting Minutes**
[...]
Vote on approval of the continued suspension of NCMFC Rule 15A NCAC 03M .0511 Bluefish.**
concerns.output_quote:
Approval of Agenda 
Approval of Meeting Minutes
[...]
• Vote on approval of the continued suspension of NCMFC Rule 15A NCAC 03M .0511 Bluefish.

concerns.analysis: In the source document on page 2, there is a nested list where 'Observer Program' and 'Incidental Take Permit Updates' are clearly sub-items indented under 'Protected Resources Update'. Similarly, 'Red Drum' and 'Southern Flounder' are sub-items under 'Landings Update'. In the JSON output, these nested hierarchies are flattened, and the sub-items are placed at the exact same level as their parent items within the 'children' array of 'Informational Materials continued:'.
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
Protected Resources Update
▪ Observer Program
▪ Incidental Take Permit Updates
concerns.output_quote: {"text": "Protected Resources Update"}, {"text": "Observer Program"}, {"text": "Incidental Take Permit Updates"}

concerns.description: Footnote(s) converted to bulleted list: 1 unordered list(s) after table(s) appear to be footnotes
concerns.analysis: footnote_as_list_count=1. Examples: 1 item(s): "Times indicated are merely for guidance. The commi". Table footnotes (often marked with *, †, ‡) were converted to <ul><li> elements instead of paragraphs. This changes the semantic meaning — screen readers announce them as list items rather than explanatory notes.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (3): Marine Fisheries Commission Business Meeting Agenda; 1; 2
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=3. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) have time values in <th> cells: "9:00 a.m."; 1 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=1, no_th_tables=1. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.

action_items: Restore the double asterisks ('**') to the appropriate agenda items to preserve 'Probable Action Items' context
Correct the list structure to reflect proper nesting for sub-items
Convert the footnote list items back to proper paragraph format
Remove retained header and footer elements
